title: fix(api/compact): typo in the remove secret handle
body:
Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27548

```release-note

```

> Not really sure how to add e2e test for this specific issue

comments.body:
Hi @axel7083 can you add a simple test for this bc apparently we dont have one?

couple of questions for the team:

1. given this is a diff path, it would be ok to fix in podman 6 as-is.  But in the case someone is using this wrong path, should we consider registering multiple paths? (@jwhonce @ashley-cui )
2. should we backport this if we do 1? (@mheon )
3. Is podman-py impacted? (ping @inknos )
in your release note, can you put the paths being corrected?
also @axel7083 thanks for filing and fixing the issue.
I think we should duplicate, instead of removing the existing handler (add another handler for `/secrets` instead of changing the existing one). We could backport that to 5.8 as well.

Do we need the duplication though? This is the compat API not libpod. So IMO there is no reason to provide docker endpoints that doesn't exists. We always reserve the right to chnage the docker API to match docker at any time. The normal podman stability rule apply only to podman/libpod API endpoints.

That said the duplication would be cheap enough so I don't mind it if that is done.
Direct API call tests for secrets go here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/apiv2/50-secrets.at

Now by default the test call the versioned endpoints, see the test Readme

> * If the endpoint has a leading slash (`/_ping`), `t` leaves it unchanged.
If there's no leading slash, `t` prepends `/v1.40`. This is a simple
convenience for simplicity of writing tests.

This bug is only on the non versioned endpoint so you must prepend `/`

Now as a general rule nobody should use the unversioned endpoints to begin with generally, one should always ask for a specific version so it doesn't surprise me it took that long to notice.
For the record, I'm fine with the rename (and not duplicating as I originally suggested) based on the last comment from @Luap99
you didn't fix the typo in the commit message though, but I am fine not blocking on that.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
